positions.thesis_summary: Highest scanner conviction (7.0) with a clear cloud capex tailwind confirmed by Google's earnings beat. Diversified cloud ETF reduces single-name risk while capturing the secular hyperscaler spend cycle.
positions.catalyst: Google earnings beat signaling accelerating cloud capex boom flowing into free cash flow across cloud infrastructure names.
positions.technical_setup: RSI 39.6 — not overbought, leaving room to run; constructive mean-reversion entry rather than chasing extended momentum.
positions.risk_factors: Cloud capex could compress near-term FCF; broad tech multiple compression if rates back up. 8% stop / 20% TP enforced.

positions.thesis_summary: Record revenue with strong AI networking/custom silicon franchise and balanced RSI offers entry without chasing. Buffett-style: durable moat, strong cash generation, dominant in AI connectivity.
positions.catalyst: Q4 2025 earnings call highlighting record revenue and AI-driven backlog growth.
positions.technical_setup: RSI 50.7 — neutral and healthiest among semis, no overbought condition, room for continuation.
positions.risk_factors: Rich valuation; AI capex digestion risk. Stop loss at 8%.

positions.thesis_summary: Provides non-semiconductor diversification (space/aerospace innovation) while still RISK-ON aligned. Outperforming JETS with higher returns and an oversold-neutral RSI gives an attractive non-correlated tilt.
positions.catalyst: Relative outperformance vs. JETS and broad innovation/aerospace momentum.
positions.technical_setup: RSI 46.9 — neutral, not extended, supports a measured entry.
positions.risk_factors: Higher-beta thematic ETF, less earnings-anchored; vulnerable in any volatility spike. Tight stop discipline applies.

positions.thesis_summary: Foundry monopoly with pristine balance sheet and the most direct, durable beneficiary of AI silicon demand. Earnings beat confirms the fundamental thesis with reasonable valuation versus peers.
positions.catalyst: TSMC earnings beat reinforcing strong AI-driven foundry demand.
positions.technical_setup: RSI 60.1 — moderately strong but not overbought, MA alignment positive in uptrend.
positions.risk_factors: Geopolitical Taiwan risk; AI bubble narrative could pressure sentiment. 8% stop enforced.

positions.thesis_summary: Alphabet offers a pristine balance sheet, dominant search/cloud franchise, and AI monetization optionality (Gemini) at a reasonable valuation. Adds mega-cap software/internet exposure that meaningfully diversifies away from the portfolio's current semiconductor concentration (AVGO/TSM/CLOU).
positions.catalyst: Strongest 1Q2026 earnings momentum cited by scanner; AI/Gemini monetization and cloud growth re-rating.
positions.technical_setup: RSI neutral at 49.1 — not overbought, leaving room to run; no momentum chase. Constructive base-building setup ahead of catalyst.
positions.risk_factors: Regulatory/antitrust overhang, AI capex spend pressuring margins, broad mega-cap tech beta.

positions.thesis_summary: Microsoft pairs Buffett-grade balance sheet quality with the strongest enterprise AI distribution via Azure and the renewed OpenAI partnership. The deeply oversold RSI (34.9) on a STRONG BUY franchise offers an attractive mean-reversion entry into a high-quality non-semi name.
positions.catalyst: Next chapter of Microsoft-OpenAI partnership; Azure AI revenue acceleration.
positions.technical_setup: RSI 34.9 — oversold on a structurally uptrending name, favorable risk/reward bounce setup. Catalyst + chart both confirm a non-momentum-chase entry.
positions.risk_factors: OpenAI restructuring uncertainty, AI capex intensity, premium valuation if growth disappoints.
